generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance SiemensSinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller enabling separate axis control with inputs for position, speed, or acceleration, and outputs for target/actual values of position, velocity, acceleration, and motor current. Interfaces with the permanent magnet synchronous motor for dynamic torque regulation."
]

instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor with adjustable current (I_M) as input and torque (τ_M) as output, interfaced with the SiemensSinumerik840DslController for dynamic torque regulation within the MABI MAX 100 first axis."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearwheel component in the cycloidal gear transmission system transforming rotational energy (τ_G → τ_out) with configurable gear ratios for precision machining applications."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component transforming rotational energy with configurable gear ratios to achieve low-speed, high-torque output for precision machining in the MABI MAX 100 first axis."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution position encoder mounted directly on the PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor shaft providing real-time angular position data (r_M) for backlash reduction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder positioned behind the CycloidalGearbox providing real-time angular position data (r_G) to enable backlash reduction by comparing with MotorPositionEncoder readings."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor current (I_M) for torque estimation via motor constants, interfaced with the SiemensSinumerik840DslController."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryPositionEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLAxisBlockDefinition : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram describing the structural composition of the MABI MAX 100 first axis including PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearWheel, and CycloidalGearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram illustrating rotational/current energy flows between the motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox components in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell providing contextual metadata including system properties, data trace origins, and semantic interoperability for the MABI MAX 100 first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AASIntegrationEnabler
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Base Simulink model representing ideal gearbox behavior without friction losses (Figure 4a) for the CycloidalGearbox component."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink implementation of Coulomb-Viscous friction model (Figure 4b) for subtracting friction losses from simulated outputs in the gear transmission system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink implementation of Stribeck friction model to account for boundary lubrication effects in the gear transmission system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink implementation of Dahl friction model representing hysteresis effects in the gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink implementation of LuGre friction model providing detailed stick-slip behavior representation in the gear transmission system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance SimModelMotor : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific Simulink simulation model for the PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor including dynamics and control characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance SimModelGearWheel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific Simulink simulation model for the GearWheel component focusing on rotational dynamics and energy transfer characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance SimModelGearbox : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific Simulink simulation model for the CycloidalGearbox including friction compensation and torque transformation characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw time-stamped motor current measurements (I_M) transmitted via MQTT from MotorCurrentSensor for torque estimation and friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance MeasuredMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw time-stamped motor angular position measurements (r_M) from MotorPositionEncoder for backlash and elasticity analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance MeasuredGearWheelPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw time-stamped gearwheel angular position measurements (r_G) from SecondaryPositionEncoder for backlash compensation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance MeasuredOutputPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw time-stamped output position measurements (r_out) from the secondary encoder behind the CycloidalGearbox for system performance validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlOptimizationService
]

instance SimulatedMotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated angular velocity data (ω̇_M) derived from MeasuredMotorPositionData using SimModelMotor with friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationDashboardEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearWheelVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated angular velocity data (ω̇_G) derived from MeasuredGearWheelPositionData using SimModelGearWheel with friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationDashboardEnabler
]

instance FrictionCompensatedOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction-compensated output position data (δr_out) derived from MeasuredOutputPositionData using all friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre)."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlOptimizationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML toolchain enabling structural (BDD) and behavioral (IBD) modeling of the MABI MAX 100 first axis components and their energy flows."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLModelBasedService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment executing all friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) and component-specific simulations (SimModel_M, SimModel_GW, SimModel_GB)."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlOptimizationService
]

instance MQTTDataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation enabling real-time sensor data streaming (I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out) from physical components to the digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingService
]

instance AASIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell implementation providing semantic interoperability and metadata management for the digital twin constellation."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboardEnabler
]

instance DSRMProcessingFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom implementation of the Digital Shadow Reference Model linking data traces, models, and sources with traceable computational steps for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingService
]

instance VisualizationDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified visualization platform displaying comparative plots of measured vs. simulated data traces for friction analysis and control tuning."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLModelBasedService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "SysML-based structural and behavioral modeling service providing the foundational digital representation of the MABI MAX 100 first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:provides AxisStructuralInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time friction identification service continuously deriving static, kinetic, and viscous friction models using data-driven and analytical approaches."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionParameterUpdateInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyImprovementAction
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model-based control optimization service integrating friction models into control algorithms via Simulink simulations to dynamically adjust torque and velocity commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlStrategyRefinementInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides TorqueVelocityAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified visualization service displaying comparative plots of measured vs. simulated data traces for friction analysis and control tuning validation."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionModelValidationInsight
]

instance DataProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Traceable data processing service enabling computational traceability of data points (e.g., how δr_M is derived from I_M and friction models)."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance AxisStructuralInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural representation of the MABI MAX 100 first axis including PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearWheel, and CycloidalGearbox components with their energy flow relationships."
]

instance FrictionParameterUpdateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of friction coefficients (Coulomb, viscous) enabling dynamic adjustment of control algorithms to maintain motion accuracy."
]

instance MotionAccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of control parameters based on friction compensation to improve motion accuracy by up to 30%."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlStrategyRefinementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insights into torque/velocity deviations (r_out vs. δr_out) informing operators to fine-tune machining parameters for higher precision."
]

instance TorqueVelocityAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of torque and velocity commands in the SiemensSinumerik840DslController based on friction-compensated simulation outputs."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparative analysis of measured vs. simulated data traces validating the accuracy of implemented friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre)."
]

instance DataTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computational lineage tracking how each data point (e.g., δr_M) is derived from raw measurements and processed through friction models."
]

// C18: Environment/Component
instance MABIMaxFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor
    base:contains GearWheel
    base:contains CycloidalGearbox
    base:contains MotorPositionEncoder
    base:contains SecondaryPositionEncoder
    base:contains MotorCurrentSensor
    base:contains SiemensSinumerik840DslController
]

instance GearTransmissionSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical gear transmission system combining GearWheel and CycloidalGearbox components transforming rotational energy with configurable gear ratios for precision machining."
    base:contains GearWheel
    base:contains CycloidalGearbox
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Custom implementation of the DSRM framework linking data traces, models, and sources with traceable computational steps for friction analysis and control optimization."
    base:contains DSRMProcessingFramework
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on a **real-time, millisecond-scale synchronization** with the physical system:
  - **Sensor data acquisition**: Continuous streaming of encoder/motor current data at high frequency (implicitly assumed for precision control; exact frequency not specified but aligned with CNC controller refresh rates).
  - **Model updates**: Friction models are **continuously recalibrated** during operation to account for wear, temperature, or load changes, enabling **dynamic adaptation** to the PT’s state.
  - **Visualization refresh**: Dashboard updates in near-real-time to reflect live friction analysis and control adjustments."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is orchestrated as a **purpose-driven digital shadow** aligned with the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**. Key components include:
  - **Purpose**: Friction derivation for motion accuracy improvement in the first axis.
  - **Asset**: MABI MAX 100’s first axis (described via SysML SystemModel).
  - **Data Sources**: Physical sensors (encoders, current sensors) and virtual processing components (SimModel_M, SimModel_GW, SimModel_GB).
  - **Processing**: Simulation-based friction compensation using black-box/white-box models, with traceable data flows from input (I_M) to output (ˆr_out).
  - **Output**: Visualized deviations between measured (r_out) and simulated (ˆr_out) data for control adjustments. The constellation is **not bidirectional**; it does not modify the PT but enables informed decision-making for operators."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the **as-operated phase** of the physical twin’s lifecycle, focusing on **real-time monitoring and adaptive control** during active machining tasks. It does not address:
  - **As-designed** (idealized) or **as-manufactured** phases, though structural models (SysML) may inform initial calibration.
  - **Post-operation analysis** (e.g., wear prediction) beyond immediate friction modeling for motion accuracy."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**:
    - **Simulink simulations** validate friction models against measured data (e.g., comparing r_out vs. ˆr_out).
    - **Parameter tuning**: Manufacturer-provided dynamics models and motor constants ensure torque/velocity calculations align with physical behavior.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**:
    - **Encoder discrepancies** (motor vs. secondary encoder) are used to infer backlash and elasticity, improving friction model accuracy.
    - **Data trace provenance**: Each data point (e.g., ˆr_G) is traceable to its source (SimModel_GW) and processing steps (friction model subtraction).
  - **Verification Mechanisms**:
    - **Dashboard visualizations** allow manual validation of simulated vs. measured data for friction patterns.
    - **No explicit statistical validation** (e.g., confidence intervals) is mentioned, but the methodology emphasizes **real-time adaptability** over offline validation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical system and digital shadow uses:
   - **MQTT protocol** for **real-time data streaming** of sensor data (input current, output angle, and derived quantities).
   - **Time-series data format**: Each data trace consists of **floating-point arrays** of torque (*τ*) and angular velocity (*ω*) values, timestamped for synchronization.
   - **No explicit network architecture details** (e.g., IP addressing, latency metrics) are provided beyond MQTT. The connection is **unidirectional** (PT → DS) with no return path."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is deployed within the **Laboratory for Machine Tools and Production Engineering (WZL), RWTH Aachen University**, using:
   - **Local infrastructure**: Hosted on lab equipment (unspecified server architecture) to support **real-time processing** and **dashboard visualization**.
   - **Executable components**: Simulink models and Processing components run on **local machines** connected to the robot via MQTT.
   - **Dashboard**: A **unified visualization tool** (implementation unspecified) displays data traces and friction analysis results in real-time."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards:
  - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: For structural and behavioral modeling of the first axis (ISO/IEC 19512).
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: ISO/IEC 62424 for semantic interoperability and metadata management in industrial systems.
  - **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)**: OASIS standard (OASIS MQTT v5.0) for real-time data streaming.
  - **Simulink**: Proprietary tool but aligned with **model-based systems engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) for multi-physics simulations."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital shadow’s **passive, one-way data flow** inherently enhances safety by:
   - **Preventing autonomous control**: No commands are sent back to the physical system, reducing risks of unintended operations.
   - **Isolated processing**: Data remains within the lab environment (WZL), with no mention of remote access or cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication). The **MQTT protocol** is assumed to follow standard security practices (e.g., TLS) if deployed in production, though this is not explicitly documented."
]
